name: Aushang — POC 4 · Double-Gated Clear-Photo Consent (no AI)

nodes.name: Consent Inputs
nodes.notes: Two INDEPENDENT flags, both default OFF in the MVP. member_photo_consent = the member opted in. admin_released_this_post = the admin released THIS post's original. The default here (consent yes, release NO) -> blurred only. Set admin_released_this_post=true to see the original released.

nodes.name: Double-Gate Consent (no AI)
nodes.notes: Pure enforcement, no AI. Original is released ONLY if member_opted_in AND admin_released — both default OFF. A client attempt to set the release is ignored. This is the privacy safety invariant, like POC 1's boundary.

nodes.name: Both gates open?
nodes.notes: TRUE = member opted in AND admin released -> mint a short-TTL signed URL to the original. FALSE (the default) = at least one gate closed -> serve only the blurred/redacted image.

nodes.name: RELEASE → signed URL to original
nodes.notes: In the MVP (src/lib/photo.ts): a short-TTL signed URL is server-minted for the original. The source_image_path column stays REVOKE'd; the client never reads it directly.

nodes.name: WITHHOLD → blurred image only
nodes.notes: The default, safe path: members see only the blurred/redacted image. The original is never exposed without BOTH explicit consents.
